pattern: Treating portal jumps as zero-cost edges turns shortest path on a grid into a 0-1 breadth-first search.
hints: Ordinary steps cost one move while teleporting between same-letter cells costs nothing, so not all edges weigh the same.
A 0-1 BFS (deque: push zero-cost moves to the front, unit moves to the back) finds the minimum number of moves.

When you first land on a letter cell, expand to every other cell of that letter at zero cost and never use that letter group again; relax the four neighbors at cost one, and read the distance to the bottom-right.

edge_inputs.description: walls force a long detour with no portals
single cell is already the goal
lone portal letter cannot teleport anywhere
two portal letters chain to skip walls

stress.description: random small grid with portals and walls, start always open
stress.note: top-left guaranteed not an obstacle per the constraint
